body:
🔴 **Design Concern** — `AzureSupportedClouds` is a closed template literal type

```ts
export type AzureSupportedClouds = `${AzureClouds}`;
```

Using a template literal union derived from an enum produces a **closed string union** — callers cannot pass custom cloud endpoint strings beyond the three hard-coded values. For extensibility, this should be typed as `string` (or `string & {}` to retain IntelliSense hints).

**Fix:** In the spec repo, adjust the TypeSpec definition so the generated type is `string`. This is the same pattern used in other ARM SDKs where `AzureSupportedClouds` is typed as `string`.

Two public API design concerns found in PR #37803 (`@azure/arm-machinelearning` 4.0.0-beta.1, TypeSpec modular rewrite from 3.0.0). CI is currently in-progress. Both findings are in the `AzureClouds`/`AzureSupportedClouds` symbols: (1) enum members use `UPPER_SNAKE_CASE` instead of PascalCase, and (2) `AzureSupportedClouds` is a closed template literal type instead of `string`. Both require spec-side fixes via `@clientName` or type adjustments in the TypeSpec repository.

<details>
<summary>📊 Structured Report</summary>

```json
{"agent":"mgmt-reviewer","pr":37803,"summary":"issues_found","findings":[{"file":"sdk/machinelearning/arm-machinelearning/review/arm-machinelearning-node.api.md","line":290,"issueType":"design","category":"naming","description":"AzureClouds enum members use UPPER_SNAKE_CASE (AZURE_CHINA_CLOUD, AZURE_PUBLIC_CLOUD, AZURE_US_GOVERNMENT) instead of PascalCase"},{"file":"sdk/machinelearning/arm-machinelearning/review/arm-machinelearning-node.api.md","line":396,"issueType":"design","category":"type-safety","description":"AzureSupportedClouds is a closed template literal type `${AzureClouds}` instead of string, preventing extensibility"}]}
```

</details>
